name: Virgin Mojito
history: The non-alcoholic version of Cuba's iconic Mojito, popularised in hotel bars across Havana for guests who wanted the experience without the rum. As beloved as the original on humid Caribbean evenings.
description: All the soul of a Mojito minus the rum — bright lime, fresh mint, and a long soda top. Effortlessly cooling.
recipe: Muddle mint leaves gently with lime juice and simple syrup in a highball glass. Fill with crushed ice. Top with chilled soda. Stir gently from the bottom. Garnish with a mint sprig and a lime wheel.

name: Pomegranate Lemonade
history: Pomegranate lemonade has roots in Persian and Mediterranean cuisines where pomegranate juice has been a staple beverage ingredient for millennia. The modern fizzy version is a brunch and summer staple.
description: Jewel-toned and tart — bright lemon balanced by sweet-tart pomegranate, finished with a lively soda lift.
recipe: Add pomegranate juice, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a shaker with ice. Shake 10 seconds. Strain into a tall glass over ice. Top with soda. Stir gently. Garnish with a lemon wheel and pomegranate seeds.

name: Shirley Temple
history: Named after the child actress Shirley Temple, invented in the 1930s at Chasen's restaurant in Hollywood (or by Royal Hawaiian bartenders, depending on whom you ask). The classic non-alcoholic 'grown-up' drink for children — and adults who want to feel young.
description: A pretty, fizzy pink classic — ginger beer's spice meeting sweet-tart pomegranate (the modern grenadine substitute) with a squeeze of lime.
recipe: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add pomegranate juice and lime juice. Top with chilled ginger beer. Stir gently. Garnish with a maraschino cherry and an orange slice if available.

name: Virgin Pomegranate Spritz
history: A modern home-bar invention designed to capture the spritz experience without alcohol — tonic's quinine adds a gentle bitter complexity that mimics aperitivo liqueurs.
description: A jewel-toned non-alcoholic spritz — pomegranate's tart sweetness brightened by lime, lifted by soda and finished with tonic's bitter botanicals.
recipe: Fill a large wine glass with ice. Add pomegranate juice, lime juice, and simple syrup. Top with soda and tonic. Stir gently. Garnish with an orange slice and pomegranate seeds.

name: Nojito Royale
history: An elevated take on the virgin mojito that adds pomegranate juice for visual drama and a deeper, slightly tart fruit note. Popular in modern bars for designated drivers who still want a celebratory experience.
description: A blush-pink mojito with attitude — mint and lime over crushed ice with a pomegranate twist. Looks like a cocktail, drinks like summer.
recipe: Muddle mint gently with lime juice and simple syrup in a highball glass. Fill with crushed ice. Add pomegranate juice. Top with soda. Stir from the bottom. Garnish with a mint crown and a lime wheel.

name: Citrus Cooler
history: A classic three-citrus cooler template that's existed since the early 20th century soda fountain era. The combination of lemon, lime, and orange produces a more complex citrus profile than any single fruit alone.
description: Brightly citrus and endlessly refreshing — three different citrus juices layered with sweet syrup and a long soda top.
recipe: Add all citrus juices and simple syrup to a shaker with ice. Shake 10 seconds. Strain into a tall glass over ice. Top with soda. Stir gently. Garnish with an orange wheel and a lemon twist.

name: Ginger Lime Fizz
history: A modern non-alcoholic riff on the Moscow Mule template — same spicy ginger, citrus, and crisp finish, just without the vodka.
description: Spicy, tart, and grown-up — ginger beer's punch with bright lime and a hint of demerara's caramel depth.
recipe: Add lime juice and demerara syrup to a copper mug or highball glass. Fill with crushed ice. Top with chilled ginger beer. Stir gently. Garnish with a fresh mint sprig and a lime wedge.

name: Cinderella
history: A vintage mocktail dating to the 1930s, originally made with pineapple, orange, and lemon juices. The 'Cinderella' name reflected the idea of a non-alcoholic drink dressed up for the ball.
description: A simple, sunny refresher — orange and lemon balanced by a touch of sweetness and a lively soda lift.
recipe: Add orange juice, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a shaker with ice. Shake hard 10 seconds. Strain into a tall glass over fresh ice. Top with soda. Garnish with an orange wedge and a maraschino cherry if available.

name: Virgin Margarita
history: The non-alcoholic Margarita has been a beach-bar fixture since the original gained popularity. Orange juice stands in for the orange liqueur, and a splash of soda adds the lift you'd otherwise get from quality tequila.
description: All the citrus snap of a Margarita without the kick — tart lime, sweet orange, balanced by syrup with a salty rim.
recipe: Optional: rim a rocks glass with salt. Add lime juice, orange juice, and simple syrup to a shaker with ice. Shake hard 10 seconds. Strain into the salted glass over fresh ice. Add a splash of soda. Garnish with a lime wheel.

name: Pomegranate Mint Cooler
history: A home-bar original combining the Middle Eastern tradition of pomegranate with the British tradition of tonic. Tonic's quinine adds a subtle bitter complexity that mimics aperitivo character.
description: Aromatic, complex, and refreshing — pomegranate's tartness meets mint's coolness with tonic's gentle bitter finish.
recipe: Slap a handful of mint between your palms to release aromas. Add to a wine glass with ice. Add pomegranate juice, lemon juice, and simple syrup. Top with chilled tonic. Stir gently. Garnish with a fresh mint sprig and pomegranate seeds.

name: Empress G&T (Virgin)
history: A non-alcoholic riff on the classic G&T template. Tonic's quinine provides the bitter botanical complexity normally added by gin, while mint and lime bring brightness.
description: Quietly sophisticated — tonic's botanical depth lifted by mint, lime, and a whisper of sweetness. The grown-up zero-proof option.
recipe: Slap mint to release aromas. Fill a large wine glass with ice. Add mint, lime juice, and simple syrup. Top with chilled tonic water. Stir gently once. Garnish with a lime wheel and a fresh mint sprig.

name: Rooh Afza Rose Cooler
history: Rooh Afza has been a beloved South Asian summer staple since 1906, traditionally served with cold water or milk during Ramadan iftars. This sparkling, citrus-lifted version is a modern home-bar update.
description: A rose-tinted, cardamom-kissed refresher — Rooh Afza's iconic floral sweetness brightened by lemon and lifted by soda.
recipe: Gently slap mint between your palms. Add to a highball glass with ice. Add Rooh Afza and lemon juice. Top with chilled soda. Stir gently. Garnish with a fresh mint sprig and a lemon slice. Optional: edible rose petals.
